questions.text: Why does the Surah begin with marketplace cheating rather than a purely ritual topic?
questions.answer: Because daily معاملات reveal real faith; cheating shows weak belief in accountability.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that small dishonest gains are harmless.
questions.answer: False. It treats cheating as a serious sin tied to denial of resurrection.

questions.text: What is one modern example of 'tatfif' (defrauding) besides weight and measure?
questions.answer: Underpaying workers, delivering low quality after charging full price, or misrepresenting products online.

questions.text: How does belief in resurrection protect a person from cheating?
questions.answer: It reminds them every act is recorded and they will stand before Allah to answer.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about records being written and ‘registered’?
questions.answer: Deeds are preserved precisely; no ظلم or good deed is lost.

questions.text: What is one lesson from the names Sijjin and Illiyyun?
questions.answer: Human lives are categorized by morality and faith; records have real destinations and outcomes.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that sins only affect actions, not the heart.
questions.answer: False. It teaches sins place ‘ran’ (rust) on the heart.

questions.text: How does the ‘ran’ on the heart develop according to this Surah’s meaning?
questions.answer: Repeated sin without repentance gradually hardens the heart until truth feels unpleasant.

questions.text: What is one practical way to remove ‘ran’ from the heart?
questions.answer: Sincere repentance, leaving the sin, increasing dhikr, and replacing sin with consistent good deeds.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about mockery of believers?
questions.answer: Mockery is a sign of arrogance; roles reverse in the Hereafter where believers are honored.

questions.text: Why do criminals mock believers according to the Surah’s portrayal?
questions.answer: They are proud of dunya standards and dislike reminders that expose their wrongdoing.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that it is fine to insult believers as long as you don’t physically harm them.
questions.answer: False. It condemns their mockery and arrogance.

questions.text: What does being veiled from seeing the Lord teach about the punishment of denial?
questions.answer: The greatest loss is spiritual: distance from Allah, not only physical punishment.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the joy and honor of the righteous in Paradise?
questions.answer: They sit on couches, observe with contentment, and drink a sealed, pure drink.

questions.text: What is one lesson from the righteous drink being ‘sealed’ and perfumed?
questions.answer: Allah’s reward is pure, protected from harm, and a special honor, unlike dunya pleasures.

questions.text: How can a business owner apply Surah Al-Mutaffifin?
questions.answer: Audit pricing and delivery honestly, correct ظلم, and treat customers and workers fairly for Allah’s sake.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about sincerity in earnings?
questions.answer: Halal earnings matter; cheating is a spiritual disease and a cause of loss in the Hereafter.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that poverty excuses cheating.
questions.answer: False. It condemns cheating and calls to accountability regardless of circumstance.

questions.text: How does Surah Al-Mutaffifin connect ethics with theology (aqeedah)?
questions.answer: It shows cheating grows from weak belief in resurrection, record-keeping, and standing before Allah.

questions.text: How can someone start reforming after realizing they have cheated others?
questions.answer: Repent to Allah, return rights where possible, apologize if appropriate, and commit to fair dealings.

questions.text: Who are the 'Mutaffifin'?
questions.answer: Those who give less in measure and weight (defrauders).

questions.text: How do the Mutaffifin act when they take measure from people?
questions.answer: They take in full.

questions.text: How do the Mutaffifin act when they give measure to others?
questions.answer: They give less than what is due.

questions.text: Do these defrauders believe they will be resurrected?
questions.answer: It warns them with a rhetorical question, reminding them they will be resurrected.

questions.text: What is the 'Great Day' mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: The Day of Judgment when mankind will stand before the Lord of the worlds.

questions.text: What is 'Sijjin'?
questions.answer: The register or the place where the record of the wicked is kept.

questions.text: How is the record of the wicked described?
questions.answer: As a 'Kitabun Marqum' (a registered book).

questions.text: What do the deniers say about the Quranic verses in this Surah?
questions.answer: They call them 'Legends of the former peoples'.

questions.text: What is the 'Ran' mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: A stain or rust that covers the hearts of people because of their sins.

questions.text: What will be the greatest 'veil' for the disbelievers on that Day?
questions.answer: They will be secluded (veiled) from seeing their Lord.

questions.text: What is 'Illiyyun'?
questions.answer: The register or the high place where the record of the righteous is kept.

questions.text: Who witnesses the record of 'Illiyyun'?
questions.answer: Those brought near to Allah (the angels).

questions.text: How will the righteous be positioned in Paradise?
questions.answer: On adorned couches, observing.

questions.text: What will the righteous be given to drink?
questions.answer: Pure wine (Rahiqun) that is sealed.

questions.text: What is the seal of the drink of Paradise?
questions.answer: Musk.

questions.text: What is 'Tasnim'?
questions.answer: A spring in Paradise from which those brought near (Al-Muqarrabun) drink.

questions.text: How did the criminals treat the believers in the worldly life?
questions.answer: They used to laugh at them, wink at each other in mockery, and call them 'astray'.

questions.text: On the Day of Judgment, who will laugh at whom?
questions.answer: The believers will laugh at the disbelievers.

questions.text: Will the disbelievers be 'repaid' for what they did?
questions.answer: Yes, they will be repaid for what they used to do.

questions.text: What is the spiritual lesson of the 'Ran' (stain) on the heart?
questions.answer: It warns that repeated sinning hardens the heart until it can no longer recognize the truth.
